reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. `[Field K] [Infinite K]` encodes "infinite field", `[AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]` encodes "vector space over K", `[Fintype ι]` with `γ : ι → Submodule K V` encodes "finitely many subspaces", and the hypothesis `γ i ≠ ⊤` correctly says each subspace is proper (strictly smaller than V). The conclusion `(⋃ i, (γ i : Set V)) ≠ ⊤` is a statement in `Set V`, where `⊤` is `Set.univ`, so this asserts that the set-theoretic union of the subspaces is not all of V — exactly "V is not the union of finitely many proper subspaces." The universal quantification over ι and γ in the Lean signature matches the universal reading of the informal claim.
